11. Sanjay Mehrotra, Micron CEO
12. Cristiano Amon, Qualcomm CEO

President Trump also says there are "many other" CEOs joining him on the trip who have not yet been disclosed.

Never in history has such a trip even remotely near this scale and caliber occurred.

This Trump-Xi meeting is far bigger than most realize.

items.title: BREAKING: US Federal government employment fell by -9,000 jobs in April, to 2.66 million, the lowest since May 1996. This marks the 14th monthly decli...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: US Federal government employment fell by -9,000 jobs in April, to 2.66 million, the lowest since May 1996.

This marks the 14th monthly decline over the last 15 months.

Since January 2025, the federal government has cut -12% of its headcount, or -345,000 jobs.

Federal jobs now account for 1.68% of total employment, the lowest percentage since at least the 1930s.

Meanwhile, the federal job openings rate fell -0.5 percentage points in March, to 2.7%, the 2nd-lowest since the 2020 pandemic low.

As a result, there were only 75,000 available vacancies in the sector, the 2nd-lowest since May 2020.

The federal workforce is contracting.

items.title: Crypto markets continue to attract massive inflows: Crypto funds posted +$858 million in inflows last week, the 6th consecutive weekly intake. Over th...
items.description_html:
Crypto markets continue to attract massive inflows:

Crypto funds posted +$858 million in inflows last week, the 6th consecutive weekly intake.

Over this period, investors poured a total of +$4.7 billion into these funds.

Crypto funds have now seen inflows in 10 of the last 11 weeks.

Bitcoin led last week’s inflows at +$706 million, bringing the year-to-date total to +$4.9 billion.

Ethereum, Solana, and XRP followed, at +$77 million, +$48 million, and +$40 million, respectively.

Meanwhile, short-bitcoin funds posted -$14 million in outflows, the largest weekly outflow of 2026.

Crypto sentiment is quickly improving.

items.title: BREAKING: Copper prices have surged to a record $6.58 per pound, now up +75% since October 2023 and over +40% in 12 months. The surge comes amid tight...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Copper prices have surged to a record $6.58 per pound, now up +75% since October 2023 and over +40% in 12 months.

The surge comes amid tight supply, declining inventories in China, and surging demand for data center construction.

Furthermore, supply disruptions at the world's 2nd-largest copper mine in Indonesia are adding to the pressure.

Meanwhile, China's exports jumped +14% YoY in April, led by booming clean-tech shipments, components that are materially copper-intensive, tightening the market even further.

The global economy is scrambling for copper.

items.description_html:
The US bond market crisis is intensifying.

While everyone is focused on AI and the Iran War, the US bond market is in a complete meltdown.

The 30Y Yield is now above 5.00% and the 10Y Yield is nearing the pivotal 4.50% level, which resulted in President Trump's "90-day tariff pause" in April 2025.

Long-term yields are now ABOVE levels seen prior to Fed rate cuts in another brutal reminder that the Fed can not contain the long-end of the yield curve.

At the current pace, we will likely see US mortgage rates rise back above 7.00% this year.

The question then becomes:

How much longer can markets (or the US government) ignore the yield crisis?

And, who folds first?

Inflation remains a major issue for Americans.

items.title: RT Maheshwer Peri: It isnt surprising that NEET exam has been cancelled by the @NTA_Exams . The hub of this entire operation is Sikar, where the succe...
items.description_html:
RT Maheshwer Peri
It isnt surprising that NEET exam has been cancelled by the @NTA_Exams . The hub of this entire operation is Sikar, where the success rate is 6X of national average. A similar thing happened in 2024 too, but the allegations were brushed under the carpet. Had we dealt with it in 2024 with an iron hand, this wouldnt have repeated.

What we know is that 140 of the 180 questions in NEET were part of a guess paper of 410 questions. What is alarming is that the order of the questions and the options were exactly the same.

The modus operandi is simple. In Sikar, the students are called in for a Mock test, a day before the actual exam and made to prepare for each of the questions in the guess paper. The students had 140 of the 180 questions preepared, thus guaranteed 600 of the 720 marks even before they entered the exam hall.

Time to go after the paper leakers. Jail them. 23 Lac students and families will pay the price of the brushing under the carpet we did in 2024. @careers360

items.title: Lots of countries announced measures early on to deal with the shortages/high prices due to #IranWar. India had earlier invoked the Essential Commodit...
items.description_html:
Lots of countries announced measures early on to deal with the shortages/high prices due to #IranWar.

India had earlier invoked the Essential Commodities Act to protect & control low LPG supply.

A (non-exhaustive) list of what others have done,

1. Philippines: Declared a state of national emergency due to the imminent danger of critically low energy supply.

2. Bangladesh: Daily fuel purchase limits by vehicle type. For eg - bikes up to 2 litres, pvt cars 10 litres etc. Shorter govt office hours. Early closure of markets & malls. And more.

3. S. Korea: Tightened public-sector vehicle rotation from five days to two days; vehicles restricted every alternate business day due to low fuel supply.

4. Indonesia: Announced fuel rationing; mandated civil servants to work from home once a week amid fuel supply disruptions and petrol-station shortages.

5. Australia: Sought agreements with Asian trade partners to secure refined fuel in return for LNG, as hundreds of service stations ran out of fuel.

6. Slovenia: Set a daily cap on road-fuel purchases at gas stations due to shortages at the pump.

#Nifty #GIFTNIFTY #BankNifty #India #IranWar‌ #Oil #Gold

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: Macquarie on SBI O-P, TP Rs 1150 4Q FY26: Earnings miss driven by weak margins and trading losses NIM impacted by higher proportion of...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
Macquarie on SBI
O-P, TP Rs 1150
4Q FY26: Earnings miss driven by weak margins and trading losses
NIM impacted by higher proportion of floating book
Asset quality remains a bright spot
Stock trades at 1.6x FY27E P/BV, which appears comfortable given expected ROEs of 15%.

JPM on SBI
OW, TP 1225
SBI reported a weaker than expected 4Q with NII (+3.8% y/y, -2% q/q) at Rs 443.8bn moderating vs +9% y/y in 3Q26 and coming 6% below JPM.
SBI recorded a 17bps q/q decline in NIM (whole bank) to 2.81% and an 18bps q/q decline in NIM (domestic) to 2.93%, vs. Street expectations of flattish to marginally lower margins q/q. However, SBI remains confident of NIM recovery in FY27 vs 4Q backed by multiple steps highlighted below.
Asset quality was benign with credit costs (on avg loans) at 24bps (-16bps q/q), although slippages (+3bps q/q) edged up slightly on seasonality.

MS on SBI
EW, TP Rs 980
Focus on call was on sharp NIM decline.
Management expects some recovery in NIM from 4Q level driven largely by loan yield recovery
Assume some recovery, yet cut F27- 28 NIM forecast by >20bp
Trim F27 & F28 EPS forecasts by 4% & 2%
Management guided to FY27 loan growth of 13-15%, assuming no material macro deterioration

CLSA on SBI
O-P TP Rs 1275
Reported a 4% beat on its 4QFY26 PBT driven by lower-than-expected opex and credit costs & a higher recovery from written-off accounts.
However, on top line, it was a weak quarter, with NII missing estimates by 5%.
This was driven by a 17bp sequential compression in NIM, sharper than expected
Calculated loan yields dropped 40bp to 7.5% driven by repo rate cuts, MCLR repricing & an increase in EBLR share.
Apart from NIM, it was a good quarter.
Loan growth of 17% YoY was best in class, while deposit growth picked up from 9% to 11% YoY
CASA ratio was steady YoY at 39%.
Slippages were steady at 0.5%, & credit costs were 0.2%.

BoFA Sec on SBI
Buy, TP Rs 1200
PAT growth of 6% YoY led by strong loan growth and lower credit cost offset by NIM compression and lower non‑interest income
NIM contraction led by repo‑linked loan repricing, MCLR resets post rate cuts, and mix shift towards T‑bill‑linked loans
FY27 guidance - 13-15% loan growth, full year NIM >3% , <50 bps credit cost and 1%+ RoA

Bernstein on SBI
Market Perform, TP Rs 1300
SBI delivered a mixed 4Q26
Loan growth accelerated to 17% YoY but NIM saw a sharp 18 bps QoQ decline to 2.93%, steepest compression amongst large banks.
Slippages rose sequentially, though largely seasonal, while headline GNPA improved to 1.49%.
RoA moderated 12 bps QoQ to 1.07%, weighed down by NIM compression & a Treasury MTM loss.
Management retained FY27 guidance of 13–15% credit growth, >3% domestic NIM & 50 bps credit costs
Management reaffirmed its through-cycle commitment to 1%+ RoA & 15%+ RoE

UBS on SBI
Neutral, TP Rs 1080
NII declined QoQ as margins dip 18bp QoQ due to loan pricing cut
Loan growth at c5% QoQ led by Corporates; asset quality stable
Management guides for loan growth of 13-15% for FY27; credit cost of c50bp
Expect bank's ROA to see downward pressure as treasury gains and credit costs normalize while margin trends remain subdued
Thus estimate ROA of 0.95% over FY27-28E (vs c1.12% in FY26) & believe risk reward at current valuations (1.2x FY27E P/BV) seems fair
Kotak Inst Eqt on SBI

Buy, TP Rs 1250
SBI reported about 5% yoy earnings growth, led by a 55% yoy decline in provisions, while operating profits declined about 10% yoy
Bank reported solid loan growth (~17% yoy), low credit costs (~25 bps) and a NIM decline of 20 bps qoq
Miss against expectations is likely to prompt a near-term pause in its re-rating, with investors awaiting evidence of recovery before rerating bank.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh

items.title: RT ValueEquity: The period of competitive advantage has shortened dramatically all over the world , Sanjoy Bhattacharya In my opinion I think this whe...
items.description_html:
RT ValueEquity
The period of competitive advantage has shortened dramatically all over the world , Sanjoy Bhattacharya

In my opinion I think this when it comes to MOAT's and in most cases assigning a higher terminal value to MOAT's may actually put you at a risk of a large de rating event when you cant forecast it , leading to irrational multiples defined by moats , until that one day it gets broken , because the rate of change and disruption to conventional MOAT's may be changing faster than ever

Some MOAT's deserve higher multiples , but we cannot assume it to perpetuity that those hold , 30 years back MOAT's were very difficult to break , now democratization of capital , tech , AI and constraints have actually turned more accessible , and we can already see conventional business models may face that terminal value risk !

video source:@moneycontrolcom

items.title: I like to talk about stocks more , when they offer larger margin of safety to longer term averages , I don't like to talk about it when they are at 20...
items.description_html:
I like to talk about stocks more , when they offer larger margin of safety to longer term averages , I don't like to talk about it when they are at 20 times book and are clearly overvalued and the hot theme

Hence when I tweet about a company today for some it is boring , but 1 year later I see so much interest in the same stock , to stay ahead of the curve reading through companies that are good companies in tough times is very important and to maintain that discipline to chase good valuations and good growth , not peak margins and peak multiples

Entry valuations is one of the single most important controllable factor , use it to make the equation in your favor !

Profit pool delta is usually at the unseen parts of the value chain , hence it is far more rewarding to look deeper in the value chain profit pools

Probabilistic nature of outcomes , survival through optionality , role of prepared luck and being antifragile Must read all these and a few more , will start sharing what I read on a weekly basis if you all are interested !

• Product design changes

A good example of how execution can offset cost pressures.

The key highlight is the changing business mix

Q4 REV grew 7% YoY, but excluding IE & IB, REV grew 13%

FY26 ex IE/IB REV grew 17%, while Wealth + Private grew 20%

Wealth + Private now contributes around 55%

This mix shift is important because these businesses are more predictable compared to Institutional Equities & Investment Banking

Asset Services has recovered fully from the Q1 disruption

Management highlighted that asset services is more like a market infrastructure business, not like a pure capital market cyclical business

The mix is shifting towards Wealth, Private Wealth, Asset Management & Asset Services

This improves predictability & reduces cyclicality

items.title: Just 2 days back, reports said Govt sources had clarified that there were no plans to raise import duties Now, the Govt has increased import duty on g...
items.description_html:
Just 2 days back, reports said Govt sources had clarified that there were no plans to raise import duties

Now, the Govt has increased import duty on gold & silver from 6% to 15%

In 2024, duties were reduced from 15% to 6% to support the jewellery ecosystem, reduce smuggling & improve formalisation.

Now, the same duty has been taken back to 15% to curb imports, reduce pressure on forex reserves & support the rupee.

The intent may be macro stability, but frequent policy reversal creates uncertainty

Existing gold & silver holders, however, would be quietly thanking the government today

items.title: Jewellery stocks were under pressure as markets were sensing that some measures could be taken to curb imports Now, with the Government announcing an ...
items.description_html:
Jewellery stocks were under pressure as markets were sensing that some measures could be taken to curb imports

Now, with the Government announcing an import duty hike, market is factoring in the possibility of some near term demand disruption due to higher gold prices

However, a few things are important to understand.

Jewellery demand in India is largely structural and cultural, driven by weddings, festivals, gifting & long term household buying

Higher duty can impact investment led gold demand more than core jewellery consumption

Historically, duty hikes have led to some moderation in imports, but they have not structurally damaged jewellery demand

Organised retailers are relatively better placed in such an environment because of brand trust, compliance, inventory management etc

A few trends can also play out from here.

Old gold exchange may increase as customers look to recycle existing gold.

Retailers may push more studded jewellery, which usually has better margins.

Valuations across the space have corrected meaningfully from highs, so a large part of the near term concern already seems to be getting factored in

Overall, this looks more like a sentiment impact in the near term

Gold & Silver in MCX up 6% today !

items.title: RT Tijori: The company is Ajinomoto Co. (TYO: 2802), and the product that matters here is Ajinomoto Build-up Film, or ABF. What makes this interesting...
items.description_html:
RT Tijori
The company is Ajinomoto Co. (TYO: 2802), and the product that matters here is Ajinomoto Build-up Film, or ABF.

What makes this interesting is the switching-cost moat. ABF is a thermosetting film that has to maintain sub-micron flatness across a 600mm sheet, and every single layer needs to be defect-free because one flaw means you scrap the entire 20-layer substrate carrying a $40,000 GPU die. Ajinomoto spent three decades dialing in the exact resin-filler chemistry, controls both the IP and the precursor amino-acid feedstock, and qualifying a new film with TSMC, Intel, or Samsung takes five to seven years of joint testing. So even if Sekisui or a Chinese competitor cracked the chemistry tomorrow, no chipmaker is going to risk a yield disaster just to switch suppliers.

The cleaner play here isn't actually Ajinomoto itself — the food business still accounts for roughly 70% of revenue, which dilutes the AI thesis and is exactly why Palliser has been pushing for a spin-off. If you want pure-play exposure, look downstream at the ABF substrate manufacturers: Unimicron, Nan Ya PCB, Kinsus, Ibiden, Shinko Electric, and AT&S.


SemiAnalysis: There is a single thin-film material required by every AI chip on earth. GPUs, TPUs, custom ASICs. All of them. 98% of global supply controlled by one Japanese chemical company. Zero production-ready alternatives. One producer fully booked through 2027. Raising prices. Lead times

The company is Ajinomoto Co. (TYO: 2802), and the product that matters here is Ajinomoto Build-up Film, or ABF.

What makes this interesting is the switching-cost moat. ABF is a thermosetting film that has to maintain sub-micron flatness across a 600mm sheet, and every single layer needs to be defect-free because one flaw means you scrap the entire 20-layer substrate carrying a $40,000 GPU die. Ajinomoto spent three decades dialing in the exact resin-filler chemistry, controls both the IP and the precursor amino-acid feedstock, and qualifying a new film with TSMC, Intel, or Samsung takes five to seven years of joint testing. So even if Sekisui or a Chinese competitor cracked the chemistry tomorrow, no chipmaker is going to risk a yield disaster just to switch suppliers.

The cleaner play here isn't actually Ajinomoto itself — the food business still accounts for roughly 70% of revenue, which dilutes the AI thesis and is exactly why Palliser has been pushing for a spin-off. If you want pure-play exposure, look downstream at the ABF substrate manufacturers: Unimicron, Nan Ya PCB, Kinsus, Ibiden, Shinko Electric, and AT&S.


SemiAnalysis: There is a single thin-film material required by every AI chip on earth. GPUs, TPUs, custom ASICs. All of them. 98% of global supply controlled by one Japanese chemical company. Zero production-ready alternatives. One producer fully booked through 2027. Raising prices. Lead times

items.title: Cipla reported a sharp deterioration in Q4 FY26 with PAT declining 54.6% YoY and EBITDA margin compressing 820 bps to 14.6%, driven by Lanreotide supp...
items.description_html:
Cipla reported a sharp deterioration in Q4 FY26 with PAT declining 54.6% YoY and EBITDA margin compressing 820 bps to 14.6%, driven by Lanreotide supply disruption and Revlimid exclusivity loss. While FY26 EBITDA margin of 20.9% met management's ~21% guidance, the company missed capex guidance significantly (10.9% vs 4-5% target).

Strong balance sheet with net cash of ₹8,872 Cr and positive FCF of ₹860 Cr provide strategic flexibility. Key catalysts for FY27 include Lanreotide re-supply expected H1 FY27, gAdvair launch, and gSymbicort preparations.

Positive Factors

India business grew 10% YoY in Q3 providing domestic growth offset

FY26 EBITDA margin of 20.9% met management's ~21% guidance

Free cash flow positive at ₹860.71 Cr despite heavy capex

Net cash position strong at ₹8,872 Cr providing strategic flexibility

CFO/PAT conversion healthy at 1.02x

gAdvair launch expected Q4 FY26 with revenue contribution in FY27

Risk Factors

Q4 PAT declined 54.6% YoY to ₹554.64 Cr driven by Lanreotide disruption and Revlimid loss

EBITDA margin collapsed 820 bps YoY to 14.6% in Q4

Revenue declined 2.8% YoY in Q4 with 7.5% QoQ decline

Lanreotide supply disruption continues with re-supply expected H1 FY27

Capex at 10.9% significantly above 4-5% guidance

US generics pricing pressure and Revlimid exclusivity loss impacted US business

items.title: Hindustan Petroleum delivered a strong Q4 with PAT up 77.6% YoY to ₹6,065 Cr, beating management's monthly guidance by 26%. Margin expanded 248 bps Y...
items.description_html:
Hindustan Petroleum delivered a strong Q4 with PAT up 77.6% YoY to ₹6,065 Cr, beating management's monthly guidance by 26%. Margin expanded 248 bps YoY to 7.22% driven by 53% GRM improvement and operating leverage.

The JV/Associate turnaround from ₹120 Cr loss to ₹1,493 Cr profit is a major inflection. Significant deleveraging (D/E from 1.30x to 0.78x) and strong cash conversion (2.10x PAT) provide solid foundation. Risks include elevated finance costs (+34.8% YoY), depreciation (+51.3% YoY), and ₹2,491 Cr forex loss.

Positive Factors

PAT grew 77.6% YoY to ₹6,065 Cr, beating monthly guidance by 26%

EBITDA margin expanded 248 bps YoY to 7.22%

JV/Associate profit turned from loss of ₹120 Cr to profit of ₹1,493 Cr

Debt reduced 23.4% YoY, D/E improved from 1.30x to 0.78x

Operating cash flow 2.10x PAT at ₹36,107 Cr

GRM improved 53% YoY to $8.79/bbl

Domestic sales volume grew 2.6% YoY to 12.43 MMT

Risk Factors

Finance costs surged 34.8% YoY due to higher borrowings and interest rates

Depreciation jumped 51.3% YoY from new assets (VRMP, projects)

Forex loss of ₹2,491 Cr impacted P&L

GRM at $8.79/bbl remains below mid-cycle target of $20/bbl

QoQ revenue declined 0.7%

items.title: TVS Motor delivered Q4 FY26 with record revenue of ₹12,808 Cr (+34% YoY) and PAT of ₹998 Cr (+31% YoY), beating its own guidance on multiple fronts ...
items.description_html:
TVS Motor delivered Q4 FY26 with record revenue of ₹12,808 Cr (+34% YoY) and PAT of ₹998 Cr (+31% YoY), beating its own guidance on multiple fronts including capex execution (18% above target) and volume targets.

EV business scaling rapidly at +51% YoY with positive contribution margins, while three-wheelers emerged as fastest-growing segment at +65% YoY. Strong cash generation (CFO/PAT 1.58x) funds the aggressive capacity expansion, though rising finance costs and NBFC subsidiary drag present near-term headwinds.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 34.1% YoY to ₹12,807.63 Cr in Q4 and 30.4% to ₹47,270.32 Cr for FY26 - highest ever annual revenue

PAT grew 33.0% YoY to ₹997.70 Cr in Q4 and 38.8% to ₹3,615.22 Cr for FY26

Total sales volume grew 28.3% YoY to 15.60 lakh units in Q4, highest ever quarterly performance

EV sales surged 51% YoY to 1.15 lakh units in Q4, with margins positive at contribution level

Capex of ₹2,013 Cr exceeded guidance by 18%, showing management confidence in growth

EBITDA margin improved 60 bps YoY normalized to 13.1% in Q4; FY margin improved to 12.9% vs 12.3%

Operating cash flow strong at ₹5,730 Cr with FCF of ₹3,717 Cr despite heavy investment cycle

Three-wheeler segment grew fastest at 65% YoY, showing new growth driver emergence

Risk Factors

Finance costs surged 64.9% YoY to ₹59.10 Cr in Q4 due to higher borrowings for capex and subsidiary investments

Consolidated operating margin declined 80 bps YoY to 11.3% vs 12.1% due to NBFC subsidiary drag

Subsidiary operations created PAT drag of ₹226 Cr (standalone PAT exceeded consolidated by this amount)

Working capital stress visible with current ratio at 0.54x for standalone (below 1.0x)

Total expenses grew 35.2% vs revenue 34.1%, showing slight negative operating leverage of ~1.1 pp

Net Debt to Equity deteriorated to 0.24x from 0.14x YoY on standalone basis

items.title: RT Exencial Research Partners: Global Copper Supply-Demand Model Copper Supply and Demand Outlook The copper market is expected to enter a period of g...
items.description_html:
RT Exencial Research Partners
Global Copper Supply-Demand Model

Copper Supply and Demand Outlook

The copper market is expected to enter a period of growing deficits due to significant supply constraints and increasing global demand.

Jefferies' supply and demand model forecasts that total global copper demand will grow at a CAGR of 2.1% from 2025 to 2030E, reaching 30,931 kt by 2030, while total supply is projected to grow at a slower CAGR of 1.7%, reaching 30,136 kt in the same period.

This imbalance is expected to result in a widening deficit, with a notional deficit of (795 kt) projected by 2030E.

At lunch, I made a comment referencing GPT. Guy next to me: "You still use GPT? Do you also still have a Hotmail account?"

items.description_html:
Swiggy is targeting contribution margin (CM) breakeven by June 2026.

Improving margins is clearly coming at the cost of growth.

Even Blinkit reported a QoQ slowdown, but still managed to grow order value by 8.1%.


Aditi Shrivastava: 🚨 Swiggy Instamart’s QoQ growth stalled with an adjusted EBITDA loss of Rs 858 crore

GOV is broadly flat
AOV falls 6% from Rs 746 to Rs 700
Dark stores added: 7 stores QoQ
Contribution margins improve

items.title: Urban Company's share price slips by 6% as losses rise in the home services business. It's InstaHelp units loss doubled QoQ to Rs 119 crore in March q...
items.description_html:
Urban Company's share price slips by 6% as losses rise in the home services business.

It's InstaHelp units loss doubled QoQ to Rs 119 crore in March quarter vs Rs 61 crore in December.

And this comes as both startup rivals Snabbit and Pronto have raised new funding rounds.


The Arc: 🚨 Urban Company reports a net loss of Rs 161 crore for the March quarter, up 57x YoY and nearly 8x QoQ.

Operating revenues up 43% YoY to Rs 426 crore.

Net transaction value (NTV) went up 42% to Rs 1,148 crore, and it reached a record 10 million orders in the quarter.

items.title: Kissht's IPO will be a multi-bagger for two of its early venture backers: VenturEast and Endiya Partners. VenturEast will make 12-13x; it held close t...
items.description_html:
Kissht's IPO will be a multi-bagger for two of its early venture backers: VenturEast and Endiya Partners.

VenturEast will make 12-13x; it held close to a 9% stake before the IPO.

Endiya will make 6x. It held 5.6% stake before the IPO.


Madhav Chanchani: 🚨 Good listing for Kissht - probably the first venture-backed consumer lender to go public.

Share price is trading nearly 20% up from the IPO price of Rs 171, market cap now stands at over Rs 3,400 crore.
